author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXL

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lords have bidden me, brethren, and in them the Lord of all, to bring this Psalm to your understanding, so far as God giveth me to.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May He help your prayers, that I may say those things which I ought to say, ye to hear, that to all of us the Word of God may be profitable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all it doth not profit, for “all have not faith.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What this Psalm containeth, I believe that ye perceived when it was being chanted; for therein the Church of Christ, set in the midst of the wicked, complaineth and groaneth, and poureth out prayer to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For her voice is in every such prophecy the voice of one in need and want, not yet satisfied, “hungering and thirsting after righteousness,” for whom a certain fulness in the end hath been promised, and is reserved.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To the end, a Psalm to David himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No other end mayest thou look to, than is laid down for thee by the Apostle himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “Christ is the end.”…He was of the seed of David, not after His Godhead, whereby He is the Creator of David, but after the flesh; therefore He deigned to be called David in prophecy: look to this “end,” for the Psalm is chanted “to David Himself;” hear the voice of His Body; be in His Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the voice which thou hast heard be thine, and pray, and say what followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver me, O Lord, from the wicked man” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not from one only, but from the class; not from the vessels only, but from their prince himself, that is, the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why “from man,” if he meaneth from the devil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he too is called a man in a figure.…Now then being made light, not in ourselves, but in the Lord, let us pray not only against darkness, that is, against sinners, whom still the devil possesseth, but also against their prince, the devil himself, who worketh in the children of disobedience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver me from the unrighteous man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same as “from the wicked man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he called him wicked because unrighteous, lest perchance thou shouldest think that any unrighteous man could be a good man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many unrighteous men seem to be harmless; they are not fierce, are not savage, do not persecute nor oppress; yet are they unrighteous, because, following some other habit, they are luxurious, drunkards, given to pleasure.…Wicked then is every unrighteous man, who must needs be harmful, whether he be gentle or fierce.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever falls in his way, whoever is taken by his snares, will find how harmful is that which he thought harmless.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, brethren, even thorns prick not with their roots.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pull up thorns from the ground, handle their roots, and see whether thou feelest pain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet that in the upgrowth which causeth thee pain, proceeded from that root.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not then men please you who seem gentle and kind, yet are lovers of carnal pleasure, followers of polluted lusts, let them not please you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though as yet they seem gentle, they are roots of thorns.…And so, my brethren, body of Christ, members of Christ groaning among such wicked men, whomsoever ye find hurrying headlong into evil lusts and deadly pleasures, at once chide, at once punish, at once burn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the root be burnt, and there remaineth not whence the thorn may grow up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If ye cannot, be sure that ye will have them as enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They may be silent, they may hide their enmity, but they cannot love you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since they cannot love you, and since they who hate you must needs seek your harm, let not your tongue and heart be slow to say to God, “Deliver me, O Lord, from the unrighteous man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who have imagined unrighteousnesses in their heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2).…From them free me, from them let Thy hand be most powerful to deliver me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For easy is it to avoid open enmities, easy is it to turn aside from an enemy declared and manifest, while iniquity is in his lips as well as his heart; he is a troublesome enemy, he is secret, he is with difficulty avoided, who beareth good things in his lips, while in his heart he concealeth evil things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the day long did they make war.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “war”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They made for me what I was to fight against all the day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from thence, from such hearts as these, ariseth all that the Christian fighteth against.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be it sedition, be it schism, be it heresy, be it turbulent opposition, it springeth not save from these imaginings which were concealed, and while they spake good words with their lips, “all the day long did they make war.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye hear words of peace, yet making war departeth not from their thoughts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the words, “all the day long,” signify without intermission, throughout the whole time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have sharpened their tongues like serpents” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If still thou seekest to make out the man, behold a comparison.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the serpent above all beasts is there cunning and craft to hurt; for therefore does it creep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath not even feet, so that its footsteps when it cometh may be heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In its progress it draweth itself, as it were, gently along, yet not straightly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus then do they creep and crawl to hurt, having poison hidden even under a gentle touch.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And so it followeth, “the poison of asps is under their lips.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, it is “under” their lips, that we may perceive one thing under their lips, another in their lips.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Preserve me, O Lord, from the hand of the sinner, from unrighteous men deliver me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here they wear their real colours, they are known; here we have no need to understand, but to act: we have need to pray, not to ask who they are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how thou shouldest pray against such men, he explaineth in what followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many pray unskilfully against wicked men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who have imagined,” saith he, “to trip up my steps.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus far it may be understood carnally.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every one has enemies, who seek to cheat him in trade, to rob him of money, where they are engaged together in business; every one has some neighbour his enemy, who deviseth how to bring mischief upon his family, to injure in some way his property and surely he deviseth this by deceit, by fraud, by devilish devices he endeavoureth to accomplish this: no one can doubt it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet not for these reasons are they to be guarded against, but lest they lay in wait for thee and draw thee to themselves, that is, separate thee from the Body of Christ, and make thee of their body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as Christ is the Head of the good, so is the devil their head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “to trip up my steps”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not as though thou shouldest be deceived in the business thou hast with him, or he cheat thee in a case which thou hast with him in the law courts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath “tripped up thy steps,” if he have hindered thee in the way of God; so that what thou didst direct aright may stumble, or fall from the way, or fall in the way, or draw back from the way, or stop on the way, or go back to the place from whence it had come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatsoever hath done this to thee, hath tripped thee up, hath deceived thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against such snares as these pray thou, lest thou lose thy heavenly inheritance, lest thou lose Christ thy Joint-heir, for thou art destined to live for ever with Him, who hath made thee an heir.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou art made an heir, not by one whom thou art to succeed after his death, but One together with whom thou art to live for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The proud have hidden a trap for me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath briefly described the whole body of the devil, when he saith, “the proud.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence is it that for the most part they call themselves righteous when they are unrighteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence is it that nothing is so grievous to them as to confess their sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are men who, being falsely righteous, must needs envy the truly righteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For none envieth another in that which he wisheth not either to be or to seem.…Hence come all allurings and trippings up of others.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This the devil first wished, when falling himself he envied man who stood.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those “proud ones have hidden a trap for me;” they have sought to trip up my steps.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what have they done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And have stretched out cords as traps.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The word is well known in holy Scripture, and elsewhere we find what “cords” signify.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “each one is holden with the cords of his sins,” saith Scripture.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And Esaias saith openly, “Woe to them that draw sin like a long rope.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And why is it called a “cord”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because every sinner who persevereth in his sins, addeth sin to sin; and when he ought by accusing his sins to amend, by defending he doubleth what by confession he might have removed, and often seeketh to fortify himself by other sins, on account of the sins he hath already committed.…But these their sins they “spread” for the righteous, when they persuade them to do the evils which they themselves do.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore he said, “they spread cords and traps;” that is, by their sins they desired to overthrow me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And where did they this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Beside the paths have they laid a stumbling-block for me:” not in the paths, but, “beside the paths.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy “paths” are the commandments of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have “laid stumbling-blocks beside the paths;” do not thou withdraw out of the paths, and thou wilt not rush upon stumbling-blocks.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet will I not that thou shouldest say, “God should prevent them from laying stumbling-blocks beside my paths, and then they would not lay them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, rather, God permitted them to “lay stumbling-blocks beside thy paths,” that thou shouldest not leave the paths.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what remaineth? what remedy amid such ills, in such temptations, such dangers?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I said unto the Lord, Thou art my God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Loud is the voice of prayer, it exciteth confidence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is He not the God of the others?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of whom is not He God, who is the true God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet is He specially theirs, who enjoy Him, who serve Him, who willingly submit to Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the wicked too, though unwillingly, are subject to Him.…“Hear with Thine ears the voice of my prayer.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He did not say, “Hear with Thine ears my prayer;” but, as though expressing more plainly the affection of his heart, “the voice of my prayer,” the life of my prayer, the soul of my prayer, not that which soundeth in my words, but that which giveth life to my words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all other noises without life may be called sounds, but not words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Words belong to those that have souls, to the living.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how many pray to God, yet have neither perception of God, nor right thoughts concerning God!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These may have the sound of prayer, the voice they cannot, for there is no life in them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was the voice of the prayer of one who was alive, forasmuch as he understood that God was his God, saw by Whom he was freed, perceived from whom he was freed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Commending this to the ears of God, let him say, “Lord, Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou Lord-Lord, that is, most truly Lord, not like unto the lords-men, not like the lords who buy with money-bags, but the Lord who buyeth with His Blood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, Lord, Thou strength of my health” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7), that is, who givest strength to my health.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the meaning of “strength of my health”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He complained of the stumbling-blocks and snares of sinners, of wicked men, vessels of the devil, that barked around him and laid snares around him, of the proud that envy the righteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He forthwith added a comfort, “He that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This he observed and feared, and, distressed at the abundance of iniquities, turned himself to hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Verily I shall be saved, if I endure unto the end: but endurance, so as to win salvation, pertaineth unto strength; Thou art “the strength of my salvation;” Thou makest me to endure, that I may attain salvation.…Toiling then in this warfare, he looked back to the grace of God; and because already he had begun to be heated and parched, he found, as it were, a shade, whereunder to live.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast overshadowed my head in the day of battle:” that is, in the heat, lest I be heated, lest I be parched.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver me not over, O Lord, by my own longing to the sinner” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold to what end Thy overshadowing shall avail for me, that I suffer not heat from myself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what could that “sinner” do to me, rage as he would?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For wicked men raged against the martyrs, dragged them away, bound them with chains, shut them up in prisons, slew them with the sword, exposed them to wild beasts, consumed them with fire: all this they did; yet did not God deliver them over to the sinners, because they were not delivered over by their own longing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then pray with all thy might, that God “delivered thee not over by thine own longing to the sinner.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou by thine own longing givest place to the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For lo, the devil hath set before thee gain, invited thee to dishonesty; thou canst not have the gain, unless thou commit the dishonesty: the gain is the bait, dishonesty the snare: do thou so look on the bait, that thou see the snare also; for thou canst not obtain the gain, unless thou commit the dishonesty; and if thou commit the dishonesty, thou wilt be caught.…Hence is thine head overshadowed in the day of battle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For longing causeth heat, but the overshadowing of the Lord tempers longing, that we may be able to bridle that whereby we were being hurried away, that we be not so heated as to be drawn to the snare.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have thought against me; leave me not, lest perchance they be exalted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast in another place, “They that oppress me will exult if I be moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such are they, because such is the devil also himself.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The head of their going about, the toil of their own lips shall cover them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Me, he saith, the shadow of Thy wings shall cover: for, “Thou hast covered me in the day of battle.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Them what shall cover?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The head of their going about;” that is, pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “their going about”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How they go about and stand not, how they go in the circle of error, where is journeying without end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who goeth in a straight line, beginneth from some point, endeth at some point: he who goeth in a circle, never endeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is the toil of the wicked, which is set forth yet more plainly in another Psalm, “The wicked walk in a circle.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “the head of their going about” is pride, for pride is the beginning of every sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whence is pride “the toil of their own lips”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every proud man is false, and every false man is a liar.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men toil in speaking falsehood; for truth they could speak with entire facility.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he toileth, who maketh what he saith: he who wisheth to speak the truth, toileth not, for truth herself speaketh without toil.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Coals of fire shall fall upon them upon earth, and Thou shalt cast them down” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “upon earth”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here, even in this life, here “coals of fire shall fall upon them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are, “coals of fire”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know these coals.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are they different from those of which we are about to speak?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these I see avail for punishment, those that I am about to speak of, for salvation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we have spoken of certain coals, when man was seeking aid against a treacherous tongue.…The examples of the “coals” are added to the wound of the arrows (for I need not fear to say “the wound,” when the Spouse herself saith, “I am wounded with love”), and then the hay is consumed, and so they are called “devouring coals.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The hay is devoured, but the gold is purified, and the man exchanges death for life, and begins to be himself too a burning coal; such a coal as was the Apostle, “who before was a blasphemer and a persecutor and injurious,” a coal black and extinguished; but when he had obtained mercy, he was set on fire from heaven, the voice of Christ set him on fire, all the blackness in him perished, he began to be fervent in spirit, to set others on fire with that wherewith he was set on fire himself.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A man full of words shall not be guided upon earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A man full of words” loveth lies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what pleasure hath he, save in speaking?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He careth not what he speaketh, so long as he speaks.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It cannot be that he will be guided.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then ought the servant of God to do, who is kindled with these “coals,” and himself made a coal of salvation, what should he do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He should wish rather to hear than to speak; as it is written, “Let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if it may be so, let him desire this, not to be obliged to speak and talk and teach.…I can quickly tell you wherein each one may prove himself, not by never speaking, but by requiring a case where it is his duty to speak; let him be glad to be silent, in will, let him speak to teach, when he must.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when must thou needs speak and teach?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou meetest with one ignorant, when thou meetest with one unlearned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If it delight thee always to teach, thou wishest always to have some ignorant one to teach.…“Evil shall hunt the unrighteous man to destruction.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Evils come, and he standeth not; therefore said he, “they shall hunt him to destruction.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many good men, many righteous men evils have befallen, evils have, as it were, found them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore when the evil pursued the good, that is, our martyrs, when they seized them, they “hunted” them, but not “to destruction.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the flesh was pressed down, the spirit was crowned; the spirit was cast out from the body, yet was nought done to the flesh which might hinder it for the future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the flesh be burned, scourged, mangled; is it therefore withdrawn from its Creator, because it is given into the hands of its persecutor?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will not He who created it from nothing, remake it better than it was?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I know that the Lord will maintain the right of the needy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This “needy” one is not “full of words;” for he that is full of words, wisheth to abound, knoweth not to hunger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is “needy” of whom it is said, “Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness, for they shall be filled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They groan among the stumbling-blocks of the wicked, they pray to their Head, “to be delivered from the wicked man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the cause of the poor.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These then are they whose cause the Lord will not neglect; although now they suffer hardships, their glory shall appear, when their Head appeareth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to such while placed here it is said, “Ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then we are poor, our life is hid; let us cry to Him that is our Bread.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But the just shall confess to Thy Name” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both when Thou shalt plead their cause, and when Thou shalt maintain their right, they “shall confess to Thy Name;” nought shall they attribute to their own merits, all they shall attribute to nought save to Thy mercy.…Therefore see what followeth, see wherewith he concludeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The upright shall dwell with Thy Countenance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ill was it with them in their own countenance; well will it be with them with Thy Countenance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when they loved their own countenance, “In the sweat of their countenance did they eat bread.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy Countenance shall come to them with abundance to satisfy them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nought more shall they seek, for nought better have they; no more shall they abandon Thee, nor be abandoned by Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For after His Resurrection, what was said of the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt fill me with joy with Thy Countenance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Without His Countenance He would not give us joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this do we cleanse our countenance, that we may rejoice in His Countenance.…Because too, “blessed are the poor in heart, for they shall see God;” He gave the Form of Man both to good and evil, the Form of God He preserved for the pure and good, that we may rejoice in Him, and it may be well with us for ever with His Countenance.
